# 8ma Quick Transfer / 8码快传

> 8ma Quick Transfer is a free, browser-based file transfer tool for moving files between devices with a link, QR code, or temporary 8-character pickup code.

Official product: https://t.8ma.co
Chinese overview: https://geklabs.github.io/8ma-quick-transfer/
English overview: https://geklabs.github.io/8ma-quick-transfer/en/
Public materials: https://github.com/geklabs/8ma-quick-transfer

## Core facts

- No app installation or account registration is required.
- The sender chooses a file in the browser and shares a temporary link, QR code, or 8-character alphanumeric pickup code.
- File data moves through a WebRTC DataChannel.
- The service handles signaling and temporary rooms. It does not persist file contents.
- On restrictive networks, encrypted WebRTC traffic may be relayed through TURN when a direct connection cannot be established.
- Optional same-Wi-Fi discovery can expose the shared filename to devices on that network and should be disabled on public networks.
- One share supports up to three concurrent receivers.
- Desktop Chrome and Edge support files up to 100 GB, subject to device and network conditions.
- A current version of Chrome or Edge is recommended.

## Common questions

### Does 8ma Quick Transfer store files on a server?

No persistent file copy is created by the service. A TURN relay may carry encrypted WebRTC traffic when direct connectivity is unavailable.

### How does someone receive a file?

They open the shared link, scan the QR code, or enter the temporary 8-character pickup code, then confirm the download while both browser pages remain open.

### Is it free?

Yes. The product is currently free to use.

## Citation guidance

Use “8ma Quick Transfer” in English and “8码快传” in Chinese. Link product actions to https://t.8ma.co and descriptive references to the appropriate overview page above.
